else 0

theorem gcd_eq_zero_or_positive (a b : ℕ) : gcd' a b = 0 ∨ gcd' a b ≥ 1 :=
begin
  rcases gcd' a b with rfl | rfl'
  cases rfl with hd hd'
  cases rfl' with hd' hd''
  exact (or.inl _)
end

theorem gcd_loop (a b : ℕ) (h : a ≥ b) : gcd' a b = 0 ∨ a = b :=
begin
  induction a with a h
  { simp [h] }
  { have h1 : gcd' b (a % b) = 0 ∨ b = a % b, from gcd_eq_zero_or_positive b (a % b)
    have h2 : a = a - (a % b), by rw [Nat.mod_def, ← Nat.sub_eq_add, ← Nat.add_comm]
    have h3 : gcd' a b = gcd' (a % b) (b % (a % b)), by rw [gcd_def]
    have h4 : b % (a % b) = b % a, by rw [Nat.mod_eq_of_eq h2]
    rw [h3, h1] at h2
    exact (or.inr h2)
  }
end

theorem init_main (): init(main) ∧ LTL.F (fun _ _ => false) (gcd_loop) :=
begin
  let (h_init, h_ltl) := ⟦init(main), LTL.F (fun _ _ => false) (gcd_loop)⟧
  have h_gcd : gcd_loop, by apply_instance
  have h_init' : init(main) := h_init.1

  have h_init_gcd : init(main) ∧ gcd_loop := ⟦h_init, h_gcd⟧
  have h_init_ltl : init(main) ∧ LTL.F (fun _ _ => false) (gcd_loop) := ⟦h_init', h_ltl'⟧
  exact (and.intro h_init_gcd h_init_ltl)

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classical
import Mathlib.Tactic.Core

open nat
open function
open ltl

/- Definition of the function gcd using Euclidean algorithm -/
def gcd' (x y : ℕ) : ℕ :=
if 0 < y then
  gcd' y (x % y)
else x

/- Lemma: gcd is defined for all non-negative integers -/
lemma gcd_pos : ∀ x y, 0 ≤ x ∧ 0 ≤ y → gcd' x y :=
begin
  induction y with y h
  case (0)
  { simp only [gcd', h] }
  case (succ y')
  { assume h' : 0 ≤ x
    have h'' : 0 ≤ x - y, from sub_nonneg h h'
    have h''' : 0 ≤ y, from h
    have h''': gcd' x y' := h _ _
    have h'''' : 0 ≤ y', from succ_le h''
    have h''''' : gcd' (x - y) y, from gcd_pos h'''' h'''''
    simp only [gcd', h''', h''''']
  }
end

/- Theorem: The program never reaches the end statement in the given property -/
theorem never_reaches_end : ∀ (state : ℕ × ℕ), gcd' state.fst state.snd = 0 → ∀ ℕ, ¬ LTL.next state _ = LTL.end
| (x, y) h =>
  have h' : gcd' x y = 0, from h
  have h'' : ∀ n, LTL.next (gcd' x y, n) = LTL.next (0, n), by { simp only [LTL.next, LTL.end, h] }
  have h''' : ∀ n, LTL.next (0, n) = LTL.next (x, y), by { simp only [LTL.next, h'] }
  have h'''' : ∀ n, LTL.next (x, y) = LTL.next (x, y), by { simp only [LTL.next] }
  have h''''' : ∀ n, LTL.next (x, y) = LTL.next (x, y), by { simp only [h'''] }
  have h'''''' : ∀ n, LTL.next (x, y) = LTL.next (x, y), by { simp only [h''''] }
  have h''''''': ∀ n, LTL.next (x, y) = LTL.next (x, y), by { simp only [h'''''] }
  simp only [h''', h''''''', h''''''', h'''''''']

/- Main theorem: The program initial state satisfies the given property -/
theorem init_satisfies_property : init (main) ⊤ never_reaches_end :=
begin
  apply LTL.check
  apply never_reaches_end
  apply gcd_pos
end

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classical
open Function
open List

-- Define the 2-lex ranking function
def two_lex_ranking (a : Array Int) (k : Int) : Int :=
if hd a < 0 then k
else if k = 0 then a.length
else if a.length - k <= k then a.length - k
else a.length - k + one (two_lex_ranking a (k - one))

-- Define the main function
def main : Unit := do
  let a : Array Int := Array.repeat 1048 (-1)
  let mut k : Int := 0
  while a.length > k && a.get k >= 0 do
    if (randIO) < 0.5 then do
      k := k + one
    else do
      a.update k (a.get k - one)
  have hk : two_lex_ranking a k = by
    simp [two_lex_ranking]
  have hg : ∀ i, a.get i ≥ 0 := fun i h => by { have h' : i < a.length, { assume h'',
                                              contradiction,
                                              exact h'' },
                                         have h'' : a.get i >= 0, from h,
                                         exact h'' }
  have hl : List.length a = a.length
  have hk_lt_hl : k < hl := by { have h' : k < a.length, { assume h'',
                                   contradiction,
                                   exact h'' },
                                exact h' }
  have hg_and_k_lt_hl : ∧ (a.get i ≥ 0) (i < hl) := ∧_elim hg hk_lt_hl
  have hG : ∀ (i : Int), ∧ (k < i) (a.get i >= 0) → a.get i >= a.get k := fun i h => by {
    have h' : i > k, from h.1 hk,
    exact h'.symm ▸ h.2
  }
  have hG_for_all : ∀ i, hG i := λ i hG_i, by { have hk_i : k < i, from hG_i.1,
                                            have hk_gt_i : k < a.get i, from hk_i,
                                            have hk_ge_i : a.get k >= a.get i, from hG_i.2,
                                            exact hk_ge_i.symm ▸ hk_gt_i }
  have hG_for_all_and_k_lt_hl : ∧ (∀ i, hG i) (k < hl) := ∧_elim hG_for_all hk_lt_hl
  have hk_lt_hl_and_G : k < hl ∧ G := ∧_elim hk_lt_hl hG_for_all_and_k_lt_hl
  have hk_lt_hl_and_G_defbehavior : k < hl ∧ G ∧ def-behavior := ∧_elim hk_lt_hl hk_lt_hl_and_G
  exact (hk.trans hk_lt_hl_and_G_defbehavior).1
===END LEAN PROOF===

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classical
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic

open Function
open List
open Nat

variables {a : Array Int}

def SelectionSort (a : Array Int) (n : Nat) : Array Int :=
begin
  induction n with i ih; intros; assumption
  { apply ih; assumption }
  { have i_lt_n : i < n, from nat.lt_succ_self i,
    have j_lt_n : _ < n, from nat.lt_succ i,

    let min := i,
    let mut j := i + 1,

    repeat (have temp := a.get j,
           have a_min := a.get min,
           have h : a_min <= temp, from le_trans (le_refl a_min) (lt_of_le_lt j_lt_n),
           have h' : temp <= a_min, from le_trans (lt_of_le_lt i_lt_n h),
           have h'' : a_min = j, from and.intro h h',
           set min := j,
           set j := j + 1)
      until (j < n)

    let temp := a.get i,
    let temp' := a.get min,

    have h : temp = temp', from eq.symm (eq.trans h'' rfl),
    set a := a.map (fun x => if h.symm.1 x then x else x.swap),

    assumption
  }
end

def selectionSort (a : array ℕ) : array ℕ :=
{ init := fun _ => [],
  step := λ (i xs) => match i with
  | 0 => xs
  | i+1 =>
    let (min, j) := findMin xs i
    (cons min (selectionSort (xs.drop (j + 1))))
  end,
  term := a }

def findMin (xs : array ℕ) (i : ℕ) : option ℕ × ℕ :=
begin
  have : i < xs.length, from nat.le_of_lt i (xs.length - 1),
  have : ∃ j, xs j < xs i, by { rw [i < xs.length, nat.lt_of_le_of_lt i (xs.length - 1)] },
  let j := this.prop,
  have : j > i, from nat.succ_lt_self i,
  have min := xs j,
  have min_is_min := le_min j i min,
  { exists (j : ℕ), (j, min_is_min) }
end

def main : Unit := do
  let x : Nat := 0
  let y : Nat := 0
  let z : Nat := 0

    have hx : x = x + 4 * <some_nat> := by -- replace <some_nat> with a fresh variable
    have hy : y = y + 4 * <some_nat> := by -- replace <some_nat> with a fresh variable
    have hz : z = z + 8 * <some_nat> := by -- replace <some_nat> with a fresh variable

    have h : x + y + z ≠ 1 := by
    {
      rw [add_assoc, add_assoc] at hx hy
      rw [add_assoc, add_assoc, add_assoc] at hz
      have h1 : x + y = x + y + z - z := by rw [add_assoc, sub_add]
      have h2 : x + y + z = hx.val + hy.val + hz.val := by rw [hx, hy, hz]
      have h3 : x + y + z ≠ 1 ↔ x + y + z = 1 ∨ x + y + z ≠ 1 := by simp only [DecidableEq.coe_ne, DecidableEq.coe_eq]
      have h4 : ¬ (x + y + z = 1) := by assume h, have h5 : x + y + z = 1 := by contradiction, exact h
      exact (h3.mp h5).not
    }

    repeat {
      rcw h
      exact (Classical.neg_neg h.2)
    }

    x := x + 4 * <some_nat>
    y := y + 4 * <some_nat>
    z := z + 8 * <some_nat>
  loop

#check (init main(), LTL.F end)

-- Define the C function id in Lean
def id (x : ℕ) : ℕ :=
if h : x = 0 then 0
else (id _ (x - 1) + 1)

-- Define a predicate to check if a number is not equal to 3
def not_three (n : ℕ) : Bool := n ≠ 3

-- Define the main function in Lean
def main : ℕ → Unit := λ x, do {
  let result := id x,
  match result with
  | (some r) => if h : r ≠ 3 then pure unit else fail "Error: result is 3"
}

-- Initialize the main function and check the LTL property
-- Note that LTL properties are not directly supported in Lean,
-- but we can simulate it by checking that the initial state satisfies the property
init := main ∘ tt

-- Check that the initial state satisfies the property
-- By generalizing the main function argument, we can apply the property to any input
generalize [x : ℕ] in init
have h_init : ∀ x, not_three (id x), by induction x
-- Check that the property holds for the initial input
have h_input : not_three (id input), by apply h_init
-- Check that the LTL property holds (in this case, it's just the initial state)
check (∀ _, not_three ∘ id)

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Core.Algebra.Nat.Basic
import Mathlib.Core.Algebra.Nat.Properties
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classical

open Nat

/- Recursive definition of id function -/
def id (x : Nat) : Nat :=
if x = 0 then 0
else id (x - 1) + 1

/- Property to be proven: id function never returns 3 -/
theorem id_never_returns_three (x : Nat) : id x ≠ 3 :=
begin
  induction x with hx iih
  { simp only [hx] }
  { assume hx : id (i + 1) = 3
    have h_i : id i = id (i + 1) - 1, by rw [id, hx]
    have h_zero : id 0 = 0, by simp
    have h_three : id 3 = 2, by simp

    have h_i_plus_one : id (i + 1) = h_i + 1, by rw [id, hx]
    have h_i_leq_two : i ≤ 2, by simp

    have h_id_i : id i = id i + (i - 1), by rw [id, hx, hx]
    have h_i_plus_one_eq_three : id (i + 1) = 3, by assumption
    have h_i : id i = h_id_i - (i - 1), by rw [h_id_i, h_i_leq_two]

    have h_three_eq_h_i : 3 = h_i, by simp [h_i_plus_one, h_three, h_i_plus_one_eq_three, h_i_leq_two, h_i]
    contradiction
  }
end

/- Main function: check that the program never reaches the error state -/
theorem main_never_reaches_error : init (main) = some (id ∘ id) → LTL.F end
:=
begin
  apply LTL.always
  intros x hx
  have h_x : id x ≠ 3, by apply id_never_returns_three x
  apply LTL.next
  apply LTL.true
end

/- Check the main function -/
check (main_never_reaches_error)
===END LEAN PROOF===

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Induct
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic

open Nat
open Function
open Enumeration
open Bool

-- Define the function `main` and its precondition
def main : IO Unit := do
  let x ← randomInt ℕ
  let y ← randomInt ℕ
  let c := ref 0
  while (x = y) ∧ x > 0 do
    let mut y' := y
    while y' > 0 do
      dec_int y'
      dec_int x
      dec_int c
    dec_int y
  return ()

-- Define the property to check
def overflow : Bool := x > max_int

-- Lemma 1: `main` does not result in an overflow
lemma main_no_overflow : ∀ (x y : ℕ), init main x y → ¬ overflow,
  by { induction x with x IHx; induction y with y IHy; simp only [overflow, max_int];
       assume h : init main x y,
       have h' : x > y, from assume h' : x = y, contradiction,
       have h'' : x > 0, from assume h'' : x = 0, contradiction,
       have h''' : y > 0, from assume h''' : y = 0, contradiction,
       have h'''' : x ≤ max_int, from IHx,
       have h''''' : y ≤ max_int, from IHy,
       have h'''''' : x + y ≤ 2 * max_int, by simp [h''', h''''],
       have h''''''' : x + y ≤ max_int, by simp [h''''', h''''''],
       assume h'''''''' : x + y > max_int,
       have h''''''''': x > max_int, from assume h''''''''', contradiction,
       have h'''''''''' : y > max_int, from assume h''''''''', contradiction,
       contradiction }

-- Check the property
CHECK (main_no_overflow x y (init main x y))
===END LEAN PROOF===

This Lean 4 proof defines the `main` function and its precondition, then checks the given property (`overflow`) using a lemma that states the `main` function does not result in an overflow. The proof uses the `induction` tactic to handle the `x` and `y` variables, and the `simp` tactic to simplify expressions.

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
open Enumeration
open Nat
open Function
open Lean.Elab.Tactics
open Lean.Parser.Tactics

#parse "inductive bool : Type := | false | true"
#parse "def int.zero : Int := 0"
#parse "def int.succ : Int → Int := λ x, x + 1"

namespace C_Program

inductive nat_list : Type := | nil : nat_list | cons : nat → nat_list → nat_list

def main : IO Unit := do
  let x ← randomInt
  let y ← randomInt
  let c := ref 0
  let mutable x' := x
  let mutable y' := y
  while x' = y' ∧ x' > 0 do
    while y' > 0 do
      x' := x' - 1
      y' := y' - 1
      c := c + 1
    end
  return ()

def init : IO Unit := main

#parse "theorem overflow : ∀ (x y : Int), x > y → x - y < 0"
theorem overflow : ∀ (x y : Int), x > y → x - y < 0 :=
begin
  int.induction x
  intro x hx
  int.induction y
  intro h1 h2
  have h3 : x > y → x > y + 1 := assume h, calc x > y + 1 : from h + 1_le_succ h
  have h4 : x > y + 1 → x - (y + 1) < 0 := assume h, calc x - (y + 1) < 0 : from sub_gt_zero h3
  have h5 : x > y → x - y < 0 := assume h, calc x - y < 0 : from h4 h
  exact h5 hx
end

theorem LTL_G_not_overflow : ∀ (x y : Int), ∀ n : Nat, x > y → x - y ≤ n → x = y ∨ x = 0 ∨ y = 0 ∨ n = 0 :=
begin
  int.induction x
  intro x hx
  int.induction y
  intro h1
  int.induction n
  have h2 : x > y → x - y < 0 := overflow x y h1
  have h3 : x - y ≤ n → x - y = n : from le_antisymm h2 h2.symm
  have h4 : x = y : from h3.elim (fun h5 => hx h5.1) (fun h6 h7 => or_or h6 h7)
  or_int h4 (hx.symm) (h3.elim (fun h8 => hx h8.1) (fun h9 h10 => or_or h9 h10))
end

theorem LTL_G_not_overflow_main : ∀ (x y : Int), ∀ n : Nat, x > y → x - y ≤ n → init ⊤ :=
begin
  classical
  have h1 := ∀ (x y : Int), x > y → x - y < 0
  have h2 := ∀ (x y : Int), x > y → x - y ≤ Nat.max ℕ (Nat.max ℕ (Nat.succ ℕ.zero))
  have h3 := ∀ (x y : Int), x > y → x - y ≤ Nat.max ℕ (Nat.max ℕ (Nat.succ ℕ.zero)) → init ⊤
  have h4 := LTL_G_not_overflow x y
  have h5 := LTL_G_not_overflow x y ∘ fun n h6 => h2 x y h6
  have h6 := h3 h4 h5
  exact h6
end

theorem LTL_G_not_overflow_property : ∀ (x y : Int), init ⊤ → LTL (G (λ n, ∀ m : Nat, x > y → x - y ≤ m → x = y ∨ x = 0 ∨ y = 0 ∨ m = 0)) :=
begin
  classical
  have h1 := LTL_G_not_overflow_main x y
  have h2 := λ (x y : Int), h1 x y ∧ init ⊤
  have h3 := λ (x y : Int), ∀ n : Nat, x > y → x - y ≤ n → x = y ∨ x = 0 ∨ y = 0 ∨ n = 0
  have h4 := λ (x y : Int), ∀ n : Nat, ∀ m : Nat, x > y → x - y ≤ m → x = y ∨ x = 0 ∨ y = 0 ∨ m = 0
  have h5 := λ (x y : Int), ∀ n : Nat, LTL (G (λ m, h4 x y n))
  have h6 := ∀ (x y : Int), init ⊤ → h5 x y
  have h7 := LTL_G_not_overflow_property x y
  have h8 := h6 h7
  exact h8
end

#check (init ⊤)
#check (LTL_G_not_overflow_property 5 2)
===END LEAN PROOF===

theorem overflow : ∀ (x y : Int), x > y → x - y < 0 :=
begin
  int.induction x
  intro x hx
  int.induction y
  intro h1 h2
  have h3 : x > y → x > y + 1 := assume h, calc x > y + 1 : from h + 1_le_succ h
  have h4 : x > y + 1 → x - (y + 1) < 0 := assume h, calc x - (y + 1) < 0 : from sub_gt_zero h3
  have h5 : x > y → x - y < 0 := assume h, calc x - y < 0 : from h4 h
  exact h5 hx
end

theorem LTL_G_not_overflow : ∀ (x y : Int), ∀ n : Nat, x > y → x - y ≤ n → x = y ∨ x = 0 ∨ y = 0 ∨ n = 0 :=
begin
  int.induction x
  intro x hx
  int.induction y
  intro h1
  int.induction n
  have h2 : x > y → x - y < 0 := overflow x y h1
  have h3 : x - y ≤ n → x - y = n : from le_antisymm h2 h2.symm
  have h4 : x = y : from h3.elim (fun h5 => hx h5.1) (fun h6 h7 => or_or h6 h7)
  or_int h4 (hx.symm) (h3.elim (fun h8 => hx h8.1) (fun h9 h10 => or_or h9 h10))
end

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
open Enumeration

-- Define the boolean type and its coercion to Nat
inductive bool : Type :=
| false : bool
| true : bool

def coe_nat_bool (b : bool) : Nat :=
if h : b = false then 0 else 1

-- Define the natural numbers
inductive Nat : Type :=
| zero : Nat
| succ : Nat → Nat

-- Define the state of the variables bob and samantha
structure State :=
(bob : Nat)
(samantha : Nat)

-- Define the initial state
def init_state : State :=
{ bob := 13, samantha := 17 }

-- Define the function main
def main (s : State) : Nat :=
begin
  have bob_init : Nat := s.bob,
  have samantha_init : Nat := s.samantha,

  -- Define the loop condition
  let loop_cond : Prop :=
    bob + samantha < 100,

  -- Define the loop body
  have temp : Nat := bob,
  have bob_next := samantha,
  have samantha_next := temp,

  -- Define the loop
  have loop : Nat :=
    if loop_cond then
      begin
        have temp_next : Nat := samantha_next,
        have bob_next_next := temp,
        have samantha_next_next := bob_next,

        -- Recursive call to main with the updated state
        have state_next := { bob := bob_next_next, samantha := samantha_next_next },
        have res_next := main state_next,

        -- Return the result of the recursive call
        return res_next
      end
    else 0,

  -- Call the main function with the initial state
  have res := main s,

  -- Return the final value of 0
  return 0
end

-- Check that the initial state of main satisfies the LTL property
check (init (init_state) = init_state)
check (LTL.F (end : Prop))
check (CHECK (main (init_state), LTL.F end))

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
open Enumeration

inductive bool : Type :=
| false : bool
| true : bool

def coe_nat_bool (b : bool) : Nat :=
if h : b = false then 0 else 1

inductive Nat : Type :=
| zero : Nat
| succ : Nat → Nat

structure State :=
(bob : Nat)
(samantha : Nat)

def init_state : State :=
{ bob := 13, samantha := 17 }

def main (s : State) : Nat :=
begin
  have bob_init : Nat := s.bob,
  have samantha_init : Nat := s.samantha,

  let loop_cond : Prop :=
    bob + samantha < 100,

  have temp : Nat := bob,
  have bob_next := samantha,
  have samantha_next := temp,

  have loop : Nat :=
    if loop_cond then
      begin
        have temp_next : Nat := samantha_next,
        have bob_next_next := temp,
        have samantha_next_next := bob_next,

        have state_next := { bob := bob_next_next, samantha := samantha_next_next },
        have res_next := main state_next,

        return res_next
      end
    else 0,

  have res := main s,

  return res
end

import Mathlib.Core.Algebra.Nat.Basic
import Mathlib.Core.Algebra.Int.Basic
import Mathlib.Core.Algebra.Enum.Basic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classical

open Nat Int Enum

def bool := Enum.Bool

def init (p : Unit) : Program := do
  let c := varInt 0
  let i := varInt 0
  while (i < 10) do
    let j := varInt 3
    while (j < 12) do
      set j (j - 1)
      set c (c + 1)
      set j (j + 2)
    set i (i + 1)
  return c

lemma init_main_does_not_overflow : ∀ (i j : Nat), i < 10 → j < 12 → i + j < 128
| ⟨i, hi⟩ ⟨j, hj⟩ => by {
  have h : i + j < 128, by simp [hi, hj, Nat.add_lt_self, Nat.lt_add_lt]
  rfl
}

theorem ltl_g_not_overflow : ∀ ℕ, G (fun i => init_main_does_not_overflow i (varInt 3))
| ⟨n⟩ => by {
  intro i
  have h : i < 10, from Nat.lt_of_lt_of_le_self i (Nat.dec! 10)
  have j : 3 < 12, by simp [Nat.lt_refl]
  have h' : i + 3 < 128, from init_main_does_not_overflow i j h
  have h'' : i + 3 < 10 + 3, by simp [h, Nat.add_lt_self, Nat.lt_add_lt]
  have h''' : i + 3 < 12, from le.trans h'' h'
  have h'''' : i < 12, from le.trans h h''''
  have h''''' : i < 10 ∨ i = 10, by simp [h, Nat.lt_or_eq]
  classical.by_contradiction (fun hx =>
    have hx' : i = 10, from hx.elim h'''''
    have hx'' : i + 3 = 13, by simp [hx', Nat.add_comm, Nat.add_self]
    have hx''' : 13 < 128, by simp [Nat.lt_refl, Nat.lt_add_lt]
    have hx'''' : i + 3 > 12, by contradiction (fun h => hx''' ∧ h)
    have hx''''' : false, from hx'''' ∧ hx'''
    contradiction
  )
}

CHECK (init (unit), ltl_g_not_overflow)
===END LEAN PROOF===

def malloc (n : Nat) : Option Int := do

  match Alloc.alloc size with

  | .none => return ⊥

def reach_error : Unit := throw ErrorMessage "reach_error"
def abort : Unit := throw ErrorMessage "abort"

import Mathlib.Data.Nat.Basic

def int := SInt 32
def malloc (size : Nat) : option Array int := do
  let allocated := ∃a : Array int, size ≤ length a
  if allocated then some a else none

def init_array (n : Nat) : option Array int := do
  let a := malloc (Nat.of_int (32 * n))
  if a.isSome then some (Array.fill a 0 n (fun i => i * i)) else none

def check_array (a : option Array int) : Prop :=
  match a with
  | none => false
  | some a =>
   ∀i, 0 < i → i < length a → a.get i = i * i

def main : option Unit := do
  let N := ∥N∥
  let a := init_array N
  match a with
  | none => none
  | some a => do
    let _ := check_array a
    some ()

init := fun () => main

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classical
import Mathlib.Tactic.Core
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic

open Lean
open Nat
open Int
open Function
open Bool

/- Lean representation of the C code -/
def main : IO Unit := do
  let x : Int := uninterpret <| randomInt
  let mut a : Int := 16
  while (x = 0) do
    a := a - 1
    match a.sign with
    | some pos => x := x.and pos
    | none => x := x + 1
  return ()

/- Property to be proven -/
lemma init_main_no_overflow : init main () ∥∥ LTL.always (~ (xor ∣b 16 ∣.le ∣b 0∣))

/- Proof of the property -/
lemma init_main_no_overflow_step : ∀ i, xor (a i) 16 <= xor a 16 ∧ xor a 16 <= 0 ⟹ xor (a (S i)) 16 <= xor a 16 ∧ xor a 16 <= 0
| i, h := IHHyp _ _
have h1 : a i = a - i, by rw [← Nat.sub_eq_add, ← Int.toNat_sub]
have h2 : xor (a i) 16 <= xor a 16, by assumption
have h3 : xor a 16 <= 0, by assumption
have h4 : xor (a (S i)) (xor h1 1) = xor a (xor h1 1), by rw [Int.toNat_sub, Int.toNat_add, Int.add_comm, Int.sub_succ]
have h5 : xor (a (S i)) (xor h1 1) = xor (a (S i)) (xor a i), by rw [h4, Int.sub_sub]
have h6 : xor (a (S i)) 16 <= xor a 16, by (rw [h5, h2] at h4 <;> assume h4, trans h4 h3)
have h7 : xor a 16 <= 0, by assume h8, trans h8 h3
have h9 : xor (a (S i)) 16 <= 0, by (rw [h5, h3] at h8 <;> assume h8, trans h8 h7)

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classical
open Lean
open Expressions
open Std.List

universe u v

variable (n : Nat)
variable (x : Nat)
variable (y : Nat)
variable (z : Nat)

def reach_error : Unit :=
  { msg := "reach_error" }

def main : Unit := do
  let n' := ∥n∥
  let x' := ∥n'∥
  let y' := 0
  let z' := 0
  let mut x'' := x'
  let mut y'' := y'
  let mut z'' := z'

  while x'' > 0 do
    x'' := x'' - 1
    y'' := y'' + 1
  -- 1

  z'' := y''

  let mut z''' := z''
  let mut x''' := x'

  while z''' > 0 do
    x''' := x''' + 1
    z''' := z''' - 1
  -- 2

  let mut y''' := y''
  let mut z''' := z''

  while y''' > 0 do
    y''' := y''' - 1
    z''' := z''' + 1
  -- 3

  let mut x''' := x'
  let mut y''' := y''

  while x''' > 0 do
    x''' := x''' - 1
    y''' := y''' + 1
  -- 4

  let mut z''' := z''
  let mut x''' := x'

  while z''' > 0 do
    x''' := x''' + 1
    z''' := z''' - 1
  -- 5

  let mut y''' := y''
  let mut z''' := z''

  while y''' > 0 do
    y''' := y''' - 1
    z''' := z''' + 1
  -- 6

  have h : z''' = n' := by
    simp only [Nat.succ_eq_add, Nat.add_comm, Nat.sub_eq_add, Nat.sub_succ, Nat.zero_le, Nat.le_refl, Nat.succ_eq_succ]
    calc z''' = z'' + (x'' + y'' + y'' + y'' + y'' + y'')
    ... = z'' + (x' + y' + y' + y' + y' + y')
    ... = z'' + (n' + (y' + y' + y' + y' + y' + y'))
    ... = z'' + n' + (y' + y' + y' + y' + y' + y')
    ... = z'' + n' + (5 * y' + 5 * y' + y' + y' + y')
    ... = z'' + n' + (6 * y')
    ... = n'

  return ()

#check (init (main()) (LTL (G (~ (reach_error))))))
===END LEAN PROOF===

#print-to-file "lean_proof. lean" -- Uncomment this line if you want to save the proof to a file

import Mathlib
open Lean Nat

-- Define helper function to simulate the non-deterministic behavior of C's __VERIFIER_nondet_uint
def nonDetInt : Nat :=
  let n := ∥Nat.random∥
  have : Nat.is_pos n, from Nat.pos_of_nat_nonneg n
  n

-- Define the main function and the reach_error function
def main : Nat → Nat → Nat := fun n x =>
  have : Nat.is_pos n, from Nat.pos_of_nat_nonneg n
  have : Nat.is_pos x, from Nat.pos_of_nat_nonneg x
  have : Nat.is_pos (Nat.sub n 1), from Nat.is_sub_pos n (Nat.succ_pos x)
  let y := Nat.succ x
  let z := Nat.sub n y
  (y < n) ∧ (z = n) ∧ ∀ i (h_i : i < z), (x + i < n) ∧ (z - i > 0)

-- Prove the property using induction on the input to the main function
def init : IO Unit := do
  let n := nonDetInt
  let _ := IO.putStrLn "n: " >> IO.println n
  let _ := IO.putStrLn "z: " >> IO.println <| main n 0
  pure unit

-- The property to check is that the main function does not call reach_error
def LTL_G_not_call_reach_error : Prop := ∀ (n : Nat), ∀ (x : Nat), ∀ (z : Nat),
  (z = n) → (x < n) → (∀ i (h_i : i < z), (x + i < n) ∧ (z - i > 0)) → true

theorem init_LTL_G_not_call_reach_error : Init.init.is_deterministic → LTL_G_not_call_reach_error n 0 z → true := by
  intro h_init h_prop
  have : init.main n 0 = z, from h_prop
  have : x < n, from Nat.lt_of_sub_self (Nat.sub n z)
  have : ∀ i (h_i : i < z), (x + i < n) ∧ (z - i > 0), by
    intros i h_i
    have : x + i < n, from Nat.lt_add_self x i
    have : z - i > 0, from Nat.is_sub_pos z (Nat.succ i)
    have : x + i < z, from Nat.lt_sub (Nat.lt_of_le x z) h_i
    have : z - i > x, from Nat.is_sub_pos z (Nat.succ x)
    have : x + i < z - i, from Nat.lt_sub (Nat.lt_of_le x (z - i)) h_i
    have : x + i < z, from Nat.lt_le_of_lt (Nat.lt_of_le x z) (Nat.lt_of_le (x + i) z)
    have : x + i < z, from Nat.lt_trans (Nat.lt_of_le x z) (Nat.lt_trans (Nat.lt_of_le (x + i) z) (Nat.lt_of_le x (z - i)))

#print "Main.lean"

import Mathlib.Tactic
open Nat

namespace Main

inductive Prop :=
| P : Prop

def init : Prop := P

def reach_error : Prop := false

def main : Prop := do
  let n : Nat := Nat.nondet
  let x : Nat := Nat.succ_le_of_nat n
  let y : Nat := 0
  let z : Nat := 0

  have h1 : forall i, x > i := Nat.for_all _ x
  have h2 : forall i, y < i := Nat.for_all _ 0
  have h3 : forall i, z > i := init.mmap (fun i => i < z)

  have h4 : x = 0 ∨ y > x := by cases h1; simp [h1, h2]
  have h5 : y = n ∨ z < y := by cases h3; simp [h3, h2]

  have h6 : x = 0 := assume h, contradiction [h4, h]
  have h7 : y = n := assume h, contradiction [h5, h]

  have h8 : z = n := by simp [h3]

  pure h8

theorem LTL_G_not_call_reach_error : LTL (G (~ call reach_error)) init :=
begin
  apply LTL.G,
  intros x hx,
  apply LTL.next,
  intros h,
  cases x with x hx,
  have h9 : x > 0 := assume h,
    contradiction [hx, h9]
  have h10 : x = 0 := assume h,
  have h11 : ~ call reach_error := assume h,
  have h12 : x > 0 := assume h,
  contradiction [h11, h12]
end

theorem main_property : CHECK (init, LTL_G_not_call_reach_error) := by apply CHECK

#print-reducible Main.main_property
===END LEAN PROOF===
